title: symphonia pauciflora
intro: Symphonia pauciflora, also known as the 'Yellow Silk-Cotton Tree', is a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae, native to the tropical rainforests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids and phenolic compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Symphonia pauciflora include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system, among others. Herbal preparations of Symphonia pauciflora include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, syrups, and other medicinal products.
